name: Bug: Reviewers receive empty diff, rubber-stamp PASS

task_definition.title: Bug: Reviewers receive empty diff, rubber-stamp PASS
task_definition.description: Reviewers receive an empty diff because git_ops uses dynamic main...HEAD which returns empty when main advances during task execution. Fix by capturing main's SHA at worktree creation time (base_commit), storing it in .task/metadata.json, and using get_diff_from_base(base_commit) in the review stage.

verify_commands.test: cd orchestrator && uv run pytest tests/ -x
verify_commands.lint: cd orchestrator && uv run ruff check src/
verify_commands.typecheck: cd orchestrator && uv run pyright src/

plan.title: Bug: Reviewers receive empty diff, rubber-stamp PASS
plan.analysis:
The bug is in workflow.py:282 which calls git_ops.get_diff_from_main(worktree), running `git diff main...HEAD`. Since worktrees share refs with the main repo, the `main` ref advances as other tasks merge during execution. In certain merge topologies (e.g., fast-forward merges, or when main absorbs overlapping changes), the three-dot diff against the moving `main` ref can return empty, causing all 5 reviewers to receive an empty diff and rubber-stamp PASS.

The fix pins the diff to main's SHA at worktree creation time:
1. git_ops.create_worktree() captures main's SHA before branching, returns it alongside the worktree path via a new WorktreeInfo dataclass
2. artifacts.init() accepts and persists base_commit in .task/metadata.json
3. A new git_ops.get_diff_from_base(worktree, base_commit) method replaces get_diff_from_main in the review stage
4. workflow.py threads base_commit through setup → artifacts → review

Existing tests in test_git_ops.py, test_artifacts.py, and test_workflow_e2e.py provide patterns to follow. All changes are within the orchestrator package.

steps.description: Write failing test in test_git_ops.py: test_create_worktree_returns_worktree_info — assert create_worktree returns a WorktreeInfo with .path (existing Path) and .base_commit (40-char SHA matching main's HEAD at creation time)

Add WorktreeInfo dataclass (path: Path, base_commit: str) to git_ops.py. Modify create_worktree to capture main's SHA via `git rev-parse main` before creating the worktree, and return WorktreeInfo instead of Path. Update existing test_create_worktree to use .path accessor

Write failing test in test_git_ops.py: test_get_diff_from_base — create worktree, commit a file, advance main with a separate commit, then call get_diff_from_base(worktree, base_commit) and assert the branch's changes are present in the diff
Add get_diff_from_base(worktree: Path, base_commit: str) -> str method to GitOps that runs `git diff <base_commit>...HEAD` — pinned SHA instead of moving ref
Write failing test in test_artifacts.py: test_metadata_contains_base_commit — call init() with base_commit='abc123def' and assert metadata.json contains that value under the 'base_commit' key
Modify artifacts.init() to accept optional base_commit: str | None = None parameter, store it in metadata dict when provided

Write failing test in test_workflow_e2e.py: test_review_uses_base_commit_diff — simulate main advancing during execution (in the implementer stub, add a commit to main), then verify the reviewer prompt contains the branch's actual diff (non-empty) by inspecting what build_reviewer_prompt receives

Wire base_commit through TaskWorkflow: (1) In run(), unpack WorktreeInfo from create_worktree into self.worktree and self.base_commit, (2) Pass base_commit to artifacts.init(), (3) In _review(), replace get_diff_from_main(self.worktree) with get_diff_from_base(self.worktree, self.base_commit). Update all existing e2e tests that access worktree_info.path or call create_worktree

design_decisions.decision: Use a WorktreeInfo dataclass instead of returning a tuple from create_worktree
design_decisions.rationale: A named dataclass is more readable than (Path, str) tuple unpacking, self-documenting, and extensible if we need to attach more metadata to worktrees later (e.g., branch name). Follows the existing MergeResult pattern in the same file.

design_decisions.decision: Keep get_diff_from_main as-is; add get_diff_from_base alongside it
design_decisions.rationale: get_diff_from_main may be used in other contexts or for debugging. Adding a new method avoids breaking any indirect callers. The workflow switches to get_diff_from_base, and get_diff_from_main can be deprecated later.

design_decisions.decision: Make base_commit optional in artifacts.init() with default None
design_decisions.rationale: Backward compatible — existing callers (including tests) that don't pass base_commit continue to work. The field is only written to metadata.json when provided.

design_decisions.decision: Store base_commit as an instance variable on TaskWorkflow rather than re-reading from metadata.json
design_decisions.rationale: The workflow creates the worktree and immediately has the base_commit. Re-reading from disk adds unnecessary I/O. The metadata.json storage is for crash recovery and debugging, not for the hot path.

reuse.how: WorktreeInfo follows the same dataclass pattern already in git_ops.py for returning structured results from git operations
get_diff_from_base uses the same _run() helper that get_diff_from_main and all other git operations use
New tests reuse the existing git_repo fixture for creating temporary repos with initial commits
The e2e test for base_commit wiring subclasses AgentStub to advance main during execution, reusing the entire fake infrastructure
New artifact test follows the same fixture pattern with tmp_path-based worktree
